result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would dissolve the illusion of separation.  

That might sound abstract, but so much suffering stems from the belief that we are fundamentally divided — by borders, beliefs, identities, or species. If humanity genuinely felt interconnected — not just intellectually, but viscerally — our decisions about resources, justice, and the natural world would transform. Empathy would move from an occasional choice to the foundation of our systems.  

It wouldn’t erase all problems, but it would shift the roots from which they grow.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
